findings: swarm-state.json uses atomic tmp+replace (POSIX rename) in 3 locations (bin/stampede.sh:155,665,1016). Exception: initial creation at line 851 uses plain open(w) — acceptable since no readers exist yet but inconsistent.

child-agents.jsonl appends use open(a) with O_APPEND semantics. Single JSONL lines are <PIPE_BUF so concurrent appenders are safe. Protocol (agents/stampede-commander.agent.md:695-703) explicitly documents append-only rules and reader tolerance of malformed trailing lines.
Task claiming uses atomic mv (POSIX rename) — race-safe by design.
Potential risks: (a) Orphaned .tmp files if process dies mid-write — no cleanup documented. (b) Commander agent atomic write obligation is enforced only by prompt text, not code guards.
